steps.text: Identify the sample mean and margin of error: the estimated average is $31.4$ pounds with a margin of error of $0.23$ pounds.
Compute the confidence interval bounds: lower bound $= 31.4 - 0.23 = 31.17$ pounds; upper bound $= 31.4 + 0.23 = 31.63$ pounds.
Interpret the margin of error: plausible values for the true population average lie within the interval $[31.17,\ 31.63]$ pounds. Values outside this range are not well supported by the data.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: Plausible values are between $31.17$ and $31.63$ pounds - this correctly describes the confidence interval. That confirms A is correct.
  • B: Plausible values are less than $31.17$ or greater than $31.63$ - this describes the region outside the confidence interval, which is the opposite of what a margin of error implies. Eliminated.
  • C: The average is less than $31.29$ pounds - $31.29 = 31.4 - 0.11$ is not one of the bounds and the statement ignores the upper range. Eliminated.
  • D: The average is greater than $31.29$ pounds - same issue; this truncates the interval incorrectly. Eliminated.
